name: La Serralada de Marina central — circular route
name_original: La Serralada de Marina central — recorrido circular
region_intro: Hiking around Badalona offers diverse landscapes, blending mountainous terrain with coastal paths. The region is characterized by the Parc de la Serralada de Marina, a natural park featuring forested trails, varied elevations, and panoramic views of the Mediterranean. Additionally, Badalona's coastline provides opportunities for walks along sandy beaches and promenades. This combination ensures a range of hiking experiences from inland hills to seaside strolls. Best hiking trails around Badalona The most popular hiking route is La Serralada de Marina central — circular route, a 5.8 miles (9.3 km) trail that takes 3 hours 2 minutes to complete, offering a comprehensive exploration of the natural park's interior. Another top favourite among local hikers is Cristall Beach – Barca Maria Beach loop from Badalona, a moderate 7.6 miles (12.2 km) path. This route follows the coastline, providing views of the Mediterranean Sea and passing by urban beaches. Local hikers also love the Route of the Serralada Marina, a 3.4 miles (5.5 km) trail leading through the natural park's varied terrain, often completed in about 1 hour 38 minutes. Hiking around Badalona is defined by its forested hills, coastal promenades, and historical sites within the Parc de la Serralada de Marina. The network offers options for various ability levels, from easy coastal walks to more challenging inland routes. The routes in Badalona are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from nearly 100 reviews. More than 1300 hikers have used komoot to explore Badalona's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Badalona?
A: Badalona offers a wide variety of hiking experiences, with over 80 routes recorded on komoot. These trails cater to different skill levels, from easy coastal walks to more challenging inland treks within the Parc de la Serralada de Marina.

Q: What types of landscapes can I expect on hikes in Badalona?
A: Hiking in Badalona provides a compelling blend of mountain and coastal landscapes. You can explore the forested trails and varied terrain of the &lt;strong&gt;Parc de la Serralada de Marina&lt;/strong&gt;, offering panoramic views of the Mediterranean. Alternatively, enjoy leisurely strolls along the city's scenic coastline, including the Badalona Seafront Promenade and sandy beaches.

Q: Are there easy hiking trails suitable for beginners or families in Badalona?
A: Yes, Badalona has numerous easy hiking trails perfect for beginners or families. Approximately 33 routes are classified as easy. Many coastal paths, such as those along the Badalona Seafront Promenade, offer gentle terrain and beautiful views. Within the Parc de la Serralada de Marina, there are also shorter, less strenuous options.

Q: Are there any challenging hikes for experienced trekkers in Badalona?
A: For experienced trekkers seeking a challenge, Badalona offers a few more demanding routes, with 5 trails classified as difficult. These are typically found within the mountainous &lt;strong&gt;Parc de la Serralada de Marina&lt;/strong&gt;, featuring greater elevation changes and potentially rocky sections. These routes provide a more strenuous workout and often lead to expansive viewpoints.

Q: Can I find circular hiking routes in Badalona?
A: Yes, circular routes are a popular option in Badalona. A great example is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1327912340/la-serralada-de-marina-central-circular-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Serralada de Marina central — circular route&lt;/a&gt;, which takes you through the heart of the natural park. Another coastal option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10434216" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cristall Beach – Barca Maria Beach loop from Badalona&lt;/a&gt;, offering seaside views.

Q: Are there historical sites or landmarks to explore on hikes in Badalona?
A: Absolutely. The region is rich in history. Within the Parc de la Serralada de Marina, you can visit the ancient &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/427891" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puig Castellar Iberian Settlement&lt;/a&gt;, an archaeological site offering insights into an ancient Iberian town. The 14th-century &lt;strong&gt;Monastery of Sant Jeroni de la Murtra&lt;/strong&gt; is another significant historical landmark often passed by hiking routes. Additionally, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2830271" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Iberian Site of Les Maleses&lt;/a&gt; provides more ancient history.

Q: Can I find hikes with great viewpoints in Badalona?
A: Many trails in Badalona offer stunning viewpoints, especially within the Parc de la Serralada de Marina. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3174771" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Coscollada Observation Tower&lt;/a&gt;, located on the highest hill in Badalona, provides expansive views encompassing the Pyrenees, Montseny, and the Mediterranean Sea. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/427891" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puig Castellar Iberian Settlement&lt;/a&gt; also offers magnificent defensive and territorial control views.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy the most about hiking in Badalona?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.4 stars from nearly 100 reviews. Hikers often praise the diverse landscapes, from the quiet woodlands of the Parc de la Serralada de Marina to the refreshing coastal promenades. The well-marked paths and the blend of natural beauty with historical sites are frequently highlighted as enjoyable aspects.

Q: Is Badalona a dog-friendly hiking destination?
A: Generally, dogs are welcome on many trails in and around Badalona, especially within the Parc de la Serralada de Marina. However, it's always advisable to keep dogs on a leash, particularly in protected areas or near wildlife, and to check specific regulations for individual trails or natural parks before you go.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Badalona?
A: The best time for hiking in Badalona is typically during the spring (April-May) and autumn (September-October) months. During these seasons, the weather is mild and pleasant, making for comfortable hiking conditions. Summers can be hot, especially on exposed trails, while winters are generally mild but can be cooler and wetter.

Q: Are there options for public transport to reach hiking trailheads in Badalona?
A: Badalona is well-connected by public transport, including metro and bus lines, which can take you close to various trailheads, particularly for coastal walks and some access points to the Parc de la Serralada de Marina. Checking local transport maps for specific starting points is recommended.

Q: Where can I find parking for hiking routes in Badalona?
A: Parking availability varies depending on the specific trailhead. For urban coastal walks, street parking or public car parks in Badalona are options. For trails within the Parc de la Serralada de Marina, designated parking areas are often available near popular access points, though these can fill up during peak times. It's advisable to research parking for your chosen route in advance.
tips:
El Turó de l'Home is a small hill straddling the municipalities of Badalona and Tiana.

   With an altitude of 362.5 m, it is where the mountain range begins where the highest mountains of the municipality of Badalón are located. At the foot of this mountain, there is the urbanization of Mas-Ram.

The hill of Fra Rafel is a mountain located on the border between the municipalities of Badalona and Sant Fost de Campsentelles. It is 413.2 meters high.

   It is located to the south-west of the Conreria de Montalegre, the former minor seminary of Barcelona. It is part of the Litoral range, and specifically of the Marina range, within the group called the Amigó mountain, which separates both municipalities.

   There are two theories about its name: that it comes from a friar of the Montalegre monastery, or that it is a deformation of the word perafel or perafita, which means "stone of term".

   The hill is made up of rocks of granitic or quartzodioritic composition, like other elevations in the area such as Montigalà or Puig Castellar.

La Coscollada is the highest hill in the city of Badalona. Located at 466.3 meters above sea level, the Coscollada is located between the Turó del Pi Candeler (462.2 m.) And the Jeia d'en Pujol pass (393.8 m). The hill forms the municipal boundary between Badalona and Sant Fost de Campsentelles, with the Sierra de l'Amigó mountains. At its foot is the Font de l'Amigó where the Canyet stream rises, which with a length of 6 km is the longest in Badalona.

At the top there is a forest watchtower, and from the very foot of this tower you can see the Pyrenees, Montseny, the coastal and pre-coastal mountains (with Montserrat), the sea, the plain of Barcelona, and some days until everything can be seen on the island of Mallorca.

Emblematic crossroads and meeting point for cyclists and hikers, where the main track that goes up from the Mirador de la Vallençana towards the Turó La Coscollada converge, with the trail that runs parallel to the quarry from the Turó de Les Maleses and with the path that goes down to the Font de l'Amigó.

One of the most emblematic viewpoints of the Serralada de Marina is the Mirador del Collet de la Vallençana, which is located in the valley of Vallensana, at the intersection of the BV-5011 road from Badalona to Montcada i Reixac with Camí Vell street in Montcada

   It can be accessed by public transport with the B12 bus line. This viewpoint has benches, trees and trash cans, to rest and enjoy the views over Canyet and the eastern sector of the Parque de la Serralada de Marina.
